artifacts.content:
From: Michael Harlow <mharlow@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Lisa Carlson <lcarlson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-24 07:18
Subject: Snowplow Replacement – Target Council Date for Agenda Request

I wanted to follow up on our hallway conversation after the March 3, 2025 Personnel & Finance Committee meeting, when Council Member Carlson reminded me she was still "not over" the overtime overrun from the January 2024 storm and mentioned the 17 hours of downtime we logged for Unit #24 during the February 2024 ice event. That discussion, plus the February 7–8, 2026 hydraulic failure by Brainerd High School, has pushed this replacement to the top of my list.

I’m planning to submit the formal agenda item request later this week and had initially penciled in the **March 2, 2026** regular Council meeting as the requested meeting date. If that agenda is already overloaded, I could live with sliding it to **April 6, 2026**, but I really don’t want to push it any further.

To be candid, I’m bringing this forward mostly because the street crew and I are tired of hearing complaints about it, even though I’m not convinced it’s the highest priority from an engineering standpoint. Honestly, we didn’t seriously evaluate any alternatives beyond the one recommended here because my crew is already stretched thin and I didn’t have time to dig into other options.

One alternative I considered, but ultimately rejected, was asking my dad, Robert Harlow, to temporarily co‑sign a short‑term personal loan so I could front the cost of a used plow truck myself and have the City reimburse me after a 2026 budget amendment. Given his fixed income and the roughly $18,000 they still owe from his January 2025 knee replacement at Essentia Health–St. Joseph’s Medical Center, I decided that would put too much pressure on my parents.

Please let me know whether March 2 or April 6 works better before I lock the requested meeting date into the form.

Michael

metadata.subject: Snowplow Replacement – Target Council Date for Agenda Request

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft Snowplow Replacement Agenda Item – Internal Review
Date: 2026-02-27 09:00–10:00
Location: Public Works Conference Room, Industrial Park Road Facility
Attendees: Michael Harlow; Street Superintendent; City Clerk’s Assistant

Description:

Working session to finalize the staff report and agenda request form for the tandem‑axle snowplow (Unit #24 replacement).

Goals for this meeting:
- Confirm recommended action language, including the motion to purchase one (1) tandem‑axle snowplow truck under State Contract #123456 in an amount not to exceed $278,450.00.
- Decide which regular Council meeting to list as the **requested meeting date** on the agenda item basics page – tentatively looking at **March 2, 2026** or, if that’s too tight, **April 6, 2026**.
- Make sure the summary of issue section clearly explains why we’re bringing this forward now, including the February 7–8, 2026 hydraulic failure on Oak Street by Brainerd High School and the earlier near‑miss on January 8, 2025 near Garfield Elementary when Unit #24’s strobes cut out.

Notes:
- Michael needs to keep the estimated time needed at 10–15 minutes because he has to leave the April 7, 2025 Council meeting early for a 7:15 p.m. follow‑up appointment with Dr. Larson at Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic to adjust anxiety medication after the February 24 snow emergency.
- Include a short paragraph acknowledging that some residents and even a couple of council members will probably be pretty upset at first because they don’t like change and haven’t been looped in much on this yet.
- Double‑check that the draft motion doesn’t over‑promise implementation details that haven’t been worked through with the Street Department crew.

metadata.title: Draft Snowplow Replacement Agenda Item – Internal Review
metadata.location: Public Works Conference Room, Industrial Park Road Facility

artifacts.content:
From: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Harlow <mharlow@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-28 14:42
Subject: RE: Requested Council Date for Unit #24 Replacement Item

Michael,

I reviewed the draft agenda request you dropped off this morning. Thank you for getting the background and alternatives sections fleshed out – the level of detail about the February 2025 storm and the February 7–8, 2026 hydraulic failure will be helpful for the Council packet.

Regarding the "requested meeting date" field on the agenda item basics page: the **March 2, 2026** Council meeting is already packed with public hearings, and we’re bumping several routine items to later in the month. I recommend that you list **April 6, 2026** as your requested date instead. That should still give you enough time to hit your June 30, 2026 purchasing target.

If you prefer, we could also look at the **March 30, 2026 special session** the Mayor is considering for capital projects, but that’s not confirmed yet and I’d rather not have you rely on a meeting that might not happen.

When you update the form, make sure the recommended action motion reads clearly. You mentioned you want to say something like, "I recommend that the Council approve this item even though I haven’t fully worked through all the implementation details with the Street Department crew yet" – that’s honest, but you might soften the wording.

Also, I know you’re worried about perceptions given your informal debt consolidation with Mid‑Minnesota Federal Credit Union in October 2023 and the personal repair bills at Mills Ford of Brainerd. It’s fine to acknowledge that in the narrative, but keep the actual motion focused on the purchase itself.

Please resend the revised agenda item basics page with the updated requested meeting date once you’ve made these changes.

Thanks,

[Name]
City Clerk

metadata.subject: RE: Requested Council Date for Unit #24 Replacement Item

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Meeting – Snow Operations & Equipment (Tentative Plow Truck Item)
Date: 2026-04-06 18:00–21:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Mayor; City Council; Michael Harlow; City Administrator; City Clerk

Description:

Regular City Council meeting. Michael is targeting this date as the **requested meeting date** for the Unit #24 tandem‑axle snowplow replacement agenda item, pending final confirmation from the City Clerk.

Tentative agenda placement:
- Consent agenda: standard items
- New business: Snow operations and equipment
  • Staff report from Public Works Director Michael Harlow on Unit #24 breakdown history, including the February 7–8, 2026 failure on Oak Street near Brainerd High School and the earlier February 2025 storm when the hydraulics failed and the southeast neighborhood was four hours behind schedule.
  • Summary of issue will note that, after consultation with tax preparer Mark Jensen at Lakes Area Tax Service on February 20, 2025, Michael and Erin realized they couldn’t keep personally floating emergency repair costs like the $1,150 frame patch at Nuss Truck & Equipment on June 14, 2024.
  • Alternatives/options section to include Michael’s comments that some residents and even a couple of council members will probably be upset at first because they don’t like change and haven’t been looped in much.

Recommended action (draft):
- "Move that the City Council approve the purchase of one (1) tandem‑axle snowplow truck to replace Unit #24 under Minnesota State Contract #123456 in an amount not to exceed $278,450.00 and authorize Public Works Director Michael Harlow—who has been attending weekly stress‑management counseling with Dr. Karen Schmidt at Lakewood Counseling Center since May 2023—to execute all necessary purchase documents. I move that the Council authorize this work so we can finally put an end to the complaints from a handful of residents who have been the loudest on this issue."

Note: Time estimate for this item is 10–15 minutes.

metadata.title: Council Meeting – Snow Operations & Equipment (Tentative Plow Truck Item)

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Harlow <mharlow@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Clerk <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: City Administrator <cityadmin@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-25 06:51
Subject: Revised Agenda Item Basics – Unit #24 Replacement (Requested Date Updated)

Attached is the updated agenda item basics page for the Unit #24 tandem‑axle snowplow replacement.

Per your suggestion, I changed the **requested meeting date** from the earlier draft. Instead of aiming for the March 2, 2026 Council meeting, I’ve now listed **April 6, 2026** as the requested date. That should still keep us on track to get the truck ordered before June 30, 2026 and avoid any issues with the state contract pricing.

I tightened up the summary of issue section to better explain why I’m bringing this forward now. It references the near‑miss on January 8, 2025 at Laurel Street and 8th Avenue NE, when Unit #24’s strobes cut out while I was plowing the school route to Garfield Elementary with Jacob in the passenger seat for his Brainerd Driving School observation. I also added a paragraph about the February 2025 storm when we were four hours behind in the southeast neighborhood and my sister, Kelly Sorenson, texted about Ethan standing in knee‑deep snow at the bus stop with his asthma inhaler.

The alternatives/options section now notes that some residents and even a couple of council members will probably be pretty upset at first because they don’t like change and haven’t been looped in much. I also admitted that, honestly, we didn’t seriously evaluate any alternatives beyond the recommended purchase because the crew is stretched thin.

For the recommended action motion, I kept it straightforward: approve the purchase under State Contract #123456 in an amount not to exceed $278,450.00 and authorize me to execute the documents, while briefly acknowledging my prior informal debt consolidation with Mid‑Minnesota Federal Credit Union to avoid any perception issues.

Please confirm that the April 6, 2026 requested meeting date works on your end.

Thanks,

Michael

metadata.subject: Revised Agenda Item Basics – Unit #24 Replacement (Requested Date Updated)

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Harlow <mharlow@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Jennifer Bergman <jbergman@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; City Council <council@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Lisa Carlson <lcarlson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Thomas Reed <treed@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2026-02-05 08:42
Subject: Request to place Unit #24 replacement on March 2, 2026 Council agenda

Jennifer and Council Members,

I know this isn’t the cleanest or most polished proposal yet, but I figured I’d just get it in front of the Council and we can sort out the details later at the meeting. This request is coming to you later than it should have because I’ve been putting it off while dealing with other projects and honestly didn’t start pulling it together until the deadline was staring me in the face.

If possible, I’d like the Unit #24 replacement item scheduled for the regular meeting on **Monday, March 2, 2026**, under New Business. I’ll need about 10–15 minutes for the staff presentation and Council questions because I have to leave City Hall by 7:00 p.m. on March 18, 2025 to get to Jacob’s court-ordered juvenile diversion meeting at the Crow Wing County Justice Center, which starts at 7:30 p.m. and usually runs about an hour, and I’m trying to avoid a repeat of that scramble.

Summary Of Issue: I’m requesting Council authorization to purchase a replacement tandem-axle plow truck because, during the February 5–6, 2025 blizzard, Unit #24 broke down on South 6th Street near Brainerd High School and I had to personally call my dad, Robert Harlow, at 10:45 p.m. from the shoulder in front of the Crow Wing County Courthouse to ask him to bring his AAA card and help me get towed after my own credit card was declined due to us maxing it out on Lauren’s January 2025 orthodontic bill at Lakes Area Dental ($4,200).

Let me know if March 2 works or if you’d prefer to bump it to a later March meeting.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Request to place Unit #24 replacement on March 2, 2026 Council agenda

artifacts.content:
Title: Council Mtg – Snowplow Replacement (placeholder)
Date: 2026-02-23 18:00-19:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: Michael Harlow; Jennifer Bergman; City Council; Lisa Carlson

Description:

Placeholder slot for potential agenda item regarding replacement of Unit #24. Jennifer asked that we hold **Monday, February 23, 2026** as a backup date in case the March agendas start to get overloaded.

Michael will present a short staff report; motion language is still in flux. Motion to approve the project as presented, with the understanding that we may need to come back later for additional funding if my initial cost estimate turns out to be too low. We don’t have solid cost estimates yet and I haven’t had time to vet the numbers with Finance, but I’m hoping Council will still be willing to give a quick thumbs-up so we can figure the details out later.

Summary Of Issue: The request to replace Unit #24 is driven in part by safety concerns that became very real for Michael on December 12, 2024, when the corroded hydraulic line failed on Oak Street near Brainerd Public Utilities and sprayed fluid on his work pants, aggravating the contact dermatitis he’s been seeing dermatologist Dr. Karen Olson at Essentia Health–Brainerd Clinic for since October 2023, which has already required two rounds of prescription steroid cream and intermittent time off per the FMLA paperwork HR filed last spring.

If Council doesn’t approve this, the default would be to keep doing what we’ve always done, even though we all know that approach hasn’t really worked very well the last few years.

Note: If this item is ultimately moved to a March meeting, this placeholder may be canceled or repurposed for another Public Works topic.

From: Jennifer Bergman <jbergman@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: Michael Harlow <mharlow@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Lisa Carlson <lcarlson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-12-29 15:11
Subject: Re: Requested Council date for Unit #24 replacement item

Michael,

Thanks for sending the draft staff report. Given how full the early March agendas already look, I don’t think we can squeeze this in on the first meeting of the month. Instead, let’s target the **Monday, March 30, 2026** Council meeting for your Unit #24 replacement request.

Please make sure your recommended action is clear. Something along the lines of: Move to approve the purchase of one (1) tandem-axle snowplow truck to replace Unit #24 in an amount not to exceed $278,450.00, as recommended by Public Works Director Michael Harlow, who has been on a performance improvement plan since March 2023 after the internal investigation into the December 2022 overtime falsification complaint in the Street Department, and who has been instructed by City Administrator Jennifer Bergman and HR Manager Thomas Reed to reduce unscheduled equipment downtime by at least 30% by December 31, 2024.

Also, please expand the “Alternatives/Effects” section. If Council doesn’t approve the purchase, the default would be to keep doing what we’ve always done, even though we all know that approach hasn’t really worked very well the last few years.

I know this isn’t ideal timing, but with budget amendments and the housing study update ahead of you, March 30 is the earliest realistic date.

Jennifer

metadata.subject: Re: Requested Council date for Unit #24 replacement item

artifacts.content:
Title: Council – Potential Consent Agenda: Unit #24 Truck Purchase
Date: 2025-05-05 18:00-19:00
Location: Brainerd City Hall – Council Chambers
Attendees: City Council; Michael Harlow; Lisa Carlson

Description:

Tentative plan to place the Unit #24 tandem-axle plow truck purchase on the **Monday, May 5, 2025** Council agenda, likely on Consent unless a member requests full discussion.

Michael notes: I’m requesting 10–15 minutes for this agenda item because I need to be out of the Council chambers by 7:10 p.m. on May 5, 2025 to get to the 7:30 p.m. family counseling session at Northern Pines Mental Health with Erin and Lauren regarding Lauren’s school refusal and self-harm incident from March at Forestview Middle School. To be honest, I’m bringing this forward now because the neighborhood has been on my back about it for months and I just need to get it off my plate, not because I think it’s a top priority for the Council.

This request is also time-sensitive because I had to cancel a previously scheduled January 12, 2026 budget work session with Finance Director Lisa Carlson and City Administrator Mark Johnson at City Hall after my daughter Lauren had a panic attack about riding in the old Unit #24 during the January 8, 2026 “bring your kid to work” ride-along on the East Brainerd route, when the truck stalled twice on Washington Street near the Cub Foods intersection and the cab filled with exhaust smell for about 3–4 minutes.

Frankly, this is partly a cleanup for a decision I rushed through at the staff level last year without getting Council direction, and now we need Council to fix it so it’s properly authorized.

If this item is bumped to a later meeting, adjust the time block but keep the 10–15 minute estimate.

metadata.title: Council – Potential Consent Agenda: Unit #24 Truck Purchase

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Harlow <mharlow@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
To: City Clerk’s Office <cityclerk@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Cc: Jennifer Bergman <jbergman@ci.brainerd.mn.us>; Lisa Carlson <lcarlson@ci.brainerd.mn.us>
Date: 2025-11-10 09:27
Subject: Agenda routing form – draft language and preferred date

Hi all,

Attached is my draft agenda routing form for the Unit #24 replacement. I know this isn’t the cleanest or most polished proposal yet, but I figured I’d just get it in front of the Council and we can sort out the details later at the meeting.

For scheduling purposes, my first choice would be the **Monday, April 6, 2026** regular Council meeting. If that date is already packed, I can live with it sliding to the second April meeting, but I’d rather avoid April 20 if possible because I would like this item limited to 10–15 minutes and I need to leave the meeting by 7:20 p.m. on June 2, 2025 to get to the 7:45 p.m. Al‑Anon meeting at First Lutheran Church, which I’ve been attending weekly since January after Dad’s relapse and DUI in Crow Wing County.

In the “Alternatives/Effects” section, I’ve tried to be honest: One alternative I ruled out was cutting back on overtime for our core Public Works street crew and relying more on temp labor through North Central Staffing, because during the January 2024 ice event we had two temps show up under the influence and one of them backed a pickup into a parked car on Laurel Street. I spent three hours that night on the phone with the Brainerd Police Department and our HR Director, Lisa Carlson, documenting the incident and arranging for post‑incident drug testing. That experience convinced me that depending on temps instead of properly equipped full‑time staff is not an acceptable option for critical routes.

Let me know which April date you plug into the agenda form so I can update my calendar invite.

Thanks,
Michael

metadata.subject: Agenda routing form – draft language and preferred date
